articles.title: Nebius AI Growth: Nvidia Partnership & Data Center Expansion to 16 Sites - News and Statistics - IndexBox
articles.summary: Nebius is expanding its AI infrastructure footprint through a partnership with Nvidia and plans to grow its data center network to 16 sites. The article highlights rising demand for AI compute capacity and positions Nebius as a participant in the broader buildout of AI-enabling infrastructure.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it reinforces the investment case around AI infrastructure, including semiconductors, data centers, and cloud-enablement providers. Advisors can use it to discuss how AI adoption is driving capital spending beyond software into the physical and compute layers of the technology stack.

articles.title: AI Data Centers Drive Tech Giants' Natural Gas Power Investments in 2026 - News and Statistics - IndexBox
articles.summary: Major technology companies are increasingly turning to natural gas power to support the rising electricity demands of AI-focused data centers in 2026. The trend highlights how AI infrastructure growth is reshaping energy procurement, with reliability and speed of deployment becoming critical factors alongside sustainability goals.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI investment is no longer limited to semiconductors and software; it is also driving demand across utilities, power generation, pipelines, and data center infrastructure. For advisors, the story broadens the AI investment theme into energy and industrial beneficiaries while also raising questions about regulatory risk, carbon goals, and the durability of AI-related capital spending.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: The article highlights recent advances in physical AI and robotics during National Robotics Week, focusing on research breakthroughs, new tools, and educational resources that support real-world machine intelligence. It emphasizes how AI is increasingly moving beyond software into embodied systems that can perceive, reason, and act in physical environments, with implications for automation, industry, and innovation ecosystems.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because physical AI expands the investment conversation beyond generative software into robotics, industrial automation, semiconductors, and edge computing. For advisors, it signals potential long-term opportunities in companies enabling autonomous machines, while also reinforcing the importance of monitoring capital spending cycles, commercialization timelines, and competitive positioning across the AI hardware and robotics value chain.

articles.title: Netflix AI Team Just Open-Sourced VOID: an AI Model That Erases Objects From Videos — Physics and All
articles.summary: Netflix’s AI team open-sourced VOID, a video inpainting model designed to remove objects from video while preserving scene consistency, including motion, lighting, and physical interactions. The release highlights continued progress in generative AI for video editing and content production, while also underscoring how major media platforms are contributing advanced AI tools to the broader developer ecosystem.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it shows how AI is moving beyond text and images into high-value enterprise video workflows, with potential implications for media, advertising, streaming, and creative software markets. For advisors, it reinforces the investment case that large platform companies and content ecosystems may benefit from AI-driven productivity gains, new creative tools, and broader monetization opportunities tied to advanced video generation and editing.

articles.title: How to Build Production-Ready Agentic Systems with Z.AI GLM-5 Using Thinking Mode, Tool Calling, Streaming, and Multi-Turn Workflows
articles.summary: The article explains how to build production-ready agentic AI systems using Z.AI’s GLM-5 model, highlighting capabilities such as thinking mode, tool calling, streaming responses, and multi-turn workflow orchestration. It focuses on practical implementation patterns for deploying AI agents that can reason through tasks, interact with external tools, and maintain context across user interactions.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it reflects continued progress in making AI agents more usable in real-world enterprise settings, which could accelerate adoption across software, financial services, and productivity platforms. For advisors, it signals that AI infrastructure providers are moving beyond basic chatbots toward operational systems that can automate workflows, potentially affecting technology valuations, competitive positioning, and long-term productivity trends.

articles.title: Is FTI Consulting’s (FCN) New AI Governance Leader a Signal on Its Long-Term Tech Strategy? - simplywall.st
articles.summary: FTI Consulting’s appointment of a new AI governance leader suggests the firm is formalizing how it manages AI risk, compliance, and enterprise adoption. The move may indicate a longer-term strategy to embed AI more deeply across consulting services while reassuring clients that governance and oversight remain central. For investors, it points to AI being treated not just as a productivity tool, but as a strategic capability tied to client demand and competitive positioning.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because leadership changes tied to AI governance can signal whether a company is moving from experimentation to scaled deployment. For advisors, that can help frame conversations about how professional services firms may use AI to improve margins, expand service offerings, manage regulatory risk, and strengthen competitive differentiation over time.

articles.title: Gujarat HC announces new AI policy, prohibits its use in decision making, judgement drafting - The New Indian Express
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court has introduced a new AI policy that allows limited use of artificial intelligence for administrative and research support but explicitly bans its use in judicial decision-making and judgment drafting. The policy reflects a cautious approach to AI adoption in sensitive public institutions, emphasizing human oversight, accountability, and legal integrity.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it highlights how regulators and public institutions are adopting AI selectively rather than broadly, especially in high-stakes settings. For advisors, it reinforces the investment theme that AI adoption may be strongest in assistive, workflow, and compliance-oriented applications, while more autonomous uses face slower uptake due to legal, ethical, and governance concerns.

articles.title: Is Wisconsin I‑39/90/94 Win And AI Push Altering The Investment Case For Jacobs (J)? - simplywall.st
articles.summary: Jacobs is being evaluated through two lenses: a newly awarded Wisconsin I-39/90/94 infrastructure project and its broader push into AI-related opportunities. The article suggests the transportation win supports near-term backlog and revenue visibility, while AI exposure may strengthen the longer-term growth narrative if the company can convert digital and data capabilities into higher-value services.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it highlights how traditional industrial and engineering firms may gain a valuation tailwind from both visible infrastructure spending and emerging AI-linked service demand. For advisors, Jacobs can serve as an example of an established company whose investment case is being reshaped by AI adoption rather than pure-play AI exposure alone.
